name: Women's Cotton Yukata - Asagao Morning Glory
name_ja: 女性用綿浴衣 朝顔柄
best_for: Summer festivals, fireworks viewing, ryokan stays
expert_note: Morning glory (asagao) is the quintessential summer yukata pattern. This cotton version breathes well in Japan's humid summers. The traditional indigo-on-white colorway works for all ages.
style_tip: Pair with a contrasting obi in yellow or red. Add a kinchaku bag for the complete look.

name: Women's Cotton Yukata - Goldfish Festival
name_ja: 女性用綿浴衣 金魚柄
expert_note: Goldfish (kingyo) patterns evoke the goldfish scooping game (kingyo-sukui) at Japanese summer festivals. Red and orange goldfish on white or navy backgrounds are the most traditional.
style_tip: A red or orange heko obi echoes the goldfish colors and keeps the look cohesive.

name: Women's Polyester Yukata - Retro Tsubaki
name_ja: 女性用ポリエステル浴衣 椿レトロ柄
expert_note: Furifu is known for bold, modern reinterpretations of traditional patterns. Their polyester yukata hold color better than cotton but breathe less in extreme heat. Best for evening festivals.
style_tip: Go bold with a contrasting musubi obi. Furifu designs work best with statement accessories.

name: Women's Cotton Yukata - Indigo Shibori
name_ja: 女性用藍染め絞り浴衣
best_for: Ryokan stays, traditional events, bon odori
expert_note: Shibori is a traditional Japanese resist-dyeing technique. Each piece has slight variations, making every yukata one of a kind. The indigo deepens with wear and washing.
style_tip: Keep it simple with a white or cream obi. Let the shibori pattern be the star.

name: Women's Linen-Blend Yukata - Geometric Hemp Leaf
name_ja: 女性用麻混浴衣 麻の葉柄
expert_note: Linen-blend yukata are the coolest option in high humidity. The asanoha pattern is one of Japan's oldest geometric motifs, symbolizing healthy growth. Slightly stiffer than pure cotton.
style_tip: A navy or dark green obi grounds the light linen fabric nicely.

name: Women's Yukata 3-Piece Set - Sakura Petals
name_ja: 女性用浴衣3点セット 桜柄
pros: Complete set with obi and geta
expert_note: Set purchases remove the intimidation of coordinating pieces. This bundle includes a yukata, matching heko obi, and wooden geta. Everything you need to wear a yukata properly.
style_tip: The included obi is already matched. Focus on adding a kanzashi hair ornament for the finishing touch.

name: Women's Yukata 3-Piece Set - Navy Hydrangea
name_ja: 女性用浴衣3点セット 紺紫陽花柄
expert_note: Hydrangea (ajisai) is a classic early summer motif in Japan, blooming just before yukata season begins. The navy base is forgiving for beginners worried about spills.
style_tip: Add a matching kinchaku bag in purple or lavender to complement the hydrangea theme.

name: Women's Polyester Yukata - Geometric Modern
name_ja: 女性用ポリエステル浴衣 幾何学モダン柄
expert_note: Furifu's geometric line appeals to younger wearers and anyone who wants yukata without full traditional aesthetics. The polyester holds sharp lines and bold color blocking well.
style_tip: A solid-color musubi obi in a contrasting shade makes the geometric pattern pop without competing.

name: Women's Cotton Yukata - Nadeshiko Wildflower
name_ja: 女性用綿浴衣 撫子柄
expert_note: Nadeshiko (fringed pink) is a beloved autumn-into-summer pattern that represents feminine grace. The small, scattered floral print works on all body types without overwhelming.
style_tip: A pink or coral heko obi picks up the nadeshiko flower colors naturally.

name: Men's Cotton Yukata - Navy Stripe
name_ja: 男性用綿浴衣 紺縞柄
best_for: Summer festivals, ryokan stays, bon odori
expert_note: Vertical stripes (shima) are the most traditional men's yukata pattern. Navy with white or gray stripes is versatile enough for any summer occasion from festivals to casual dining.
style_tip: Pair with a dark gray or black kaku obi. Keep the waist tie firm and the neckline open.

name: Men's Cotton Yukata - Indigo Kasuri
name_ja: 男性用綿浴衣 紺絣柄
expert_note: Kasuri is a traditional ikat-style weaving technique creating blurred geometric patterns. The subtle texture gives depth without busy patterns, preferred by men who want understated style.
style_tip: A simple black or charcoal kaku obi and wooden geta complete the classic look.

name: Men's Cotton Yukata - Black Dragonfly
name_ja: 男性用綿浴衣 黒蜻蛉柄
expert_note: Dragonflies (tonbo) symbolize courage and strength in Japanese culture, as they only fly forward. A black base with subtle dragonfly motifs gives a sophisticated, masculine look.
style_tip: A dark gray kaku obi keeps the monochrome theme. Add sensu folding fan for a refined touch.

name: Men's Jinbei - Indigo Waffle Weave
name_ja: 男性用甚平 紺ワッフル織
best_for: Casual home wear, neighborhood festivals, relaxation
expert_note: Jinbei are a two-piece alternative to yukata consisting of a top and shorts. Much easier to wear than a full yukata with no obi tying required. The waffle weave maximizes airflow.
style_tip: Jinbei are inherently casual. Wear with simple wooden geta or even barefoot at home.

name: Men's Yukata 3-Piece Set - Charcoal Lattice
name_ja: 男性用浴衣3点セット 墨格子柄
pros: Complete set with obi and geta
best_for: First-time yukata buyers, summer festival beginners
expert_note: This set includes a yukata, kaku obi, and geta. The lattice pattern is understated and works for all ages. The included kaku obi is pre-cut to the right length for easy tying.
style_tip: Practice the simple kaku obi knot (kainokuchi musubi) a few times before the event.

name: Uniqlo Women's Yukata - Minimalist Floral
name_ja: ユニクロ レディース浴衣 花柄
expert_note: Uniqlo releases a limited yukata line each summer (typically June-August). Their designs lean minimalist and modern. Quality cotton at an accessible price point, though selection varies by year.
style_tip: Pair with Uniqlo's matching obi belt sold separately. Their sizing runs slightly larger than traditional brands.

name: Kids' Cotton Yukata - Kabuto Helmet
name_ja: 子供用綿浴衣 兜柄
expert_note: Kids' yukata sizes are based on height in centimeters, not age. Measure your child's height and add 5-10cm for growing room. The kabuto pattern is popular for boys' summer festivals.
style_tip: A simple heko obi in a matching color is easiest for kids. Velcro closures are also available for very young children.

name: Kids' Cotton Yukata - Usagi Rabbit
name_ja: 子供用綿浴衣 うさぎ柄
expert_note: Rabbit (usagi) patterns are gender-neutral and popular across all ages in Japan. The playful hopping rabbit motif connects to the Japanese moon rabbit folklore, a fun story to share with kids.
style_tip: A pastel-colored heko obi in pink or light blue complements the rabbit theme.

name: Women's Heko Obi - Sheer Organza
name_ja: 女性用兵児帯 オーガンジー
expert_note: Heko obi are the easiest obi type for beginners. Simply wrap and tie a bow at the back. The sheer organza adds volume to the bow without bulk. Multiple wraps create a fuller look.
style_tip: Layer two colors of heko obi for a richer, more dimensional look at the back.

name: Women's Heko Obi - Cotton Gauze
name_ja: 女性用兵児帯 綿ガーゼ
best_for: Casual summer outings, home yukata wear
expert_note: Cotton gauze heko obi are softer than organza but create a more relaxed, less structured bow. The natural cotton breathes well and feels less slippery than polyester during tying.
style_tip: Wrap twice around the waist for stability, then tie a simple ribbon bow.

name: Women's Musubi Obi - Pre-Tied Butterfly
name_ja: 女性用作り帯 蝶結び
best_for: Absolute beginners, travelers, anyone intimidated by obi tying
expert_note: Musubi (tsukuri) obi come in two pieces: a flat wrap for the waist and a pre-shaped decorative bow that clips on the back. They eliminate the most intimidating part of wearing yukata.
style_tip: Position the pre-tied bow slightly off-center to the left of your spine for the most natural look.

name: Men's Kaku Obi - Dark Gray Cotton
name_ja: 男性用角帯 濃灰綿
expert_note: Kaku obi are the standard men's obi, about 10cm wide. The stiff weave holds the kainokuchi musubi (shellfish knot) without slipping. Dark gray matches almost any men's yukata color.
style_tip: The knot should sit at the lower back, slightly below the waist. Tighter is more comfortable than too loose.

name: Women's Paulownia Geta - Natural Finish
name_ja: 女性用桐下駄 ナチュラル
expert_note: Paulownia (kiri) wood is the traditional material for geta because it is extremely light and absorbs moisture from bare feet. The two-tooth (ni-ha) style is the most classic shape.
style_tip: Geta should be slightly smaller than your foot, with the heel hanging off the back by 1-2cm. This is correct fit, not too small.

name: Women's Right-Angle Geta - Lacquered Black
name_ja: 女性用角下駄 黒塗り
pros: Easier to walk in than two-tooth
best_for: Extended walking at festivals, paved surfaces
expert_note: Right-angle (kaku) geta have a flat bottom instead of two teeth, making them more stable on modern paved surfaces. The lacquer finish adds elegance and protects the wood from moisture.
style_tip: Black lacquer geta are more formal. Pair with a polished look for fireworks viewing or dinner.

name: Men's Paulownia Geta - Traditional Two-Tooth
name_ja: 男性用桐下駄 二歯
best_for: Festival wear, yukata and jinbei pairing
expert_note: Men's geta are wider and have thicker teeth than women's. The two-tooth design takes some practice to walk in comfortably. Start on flat surfaces before navigating festival crowds.
style_tip: Dark-colored thong straps (hanao) are standard for men. Navy or black pair with any yukata.

name: Unisex Setta Sandals - Rush Grass
name_ja: 雪駄 い草表
pros: Works with both yukata and Western clothes
expert_note: Setta are flat sandals with a woven rush grass (igusa) top surface, cooler and easier to walk in than raised geta. The leather or rubber sole provides good grip on modern surfaces.
style_tip: Setta bridge traditional and modern styling. They work equally well with yukata, jinbei, or shorts and a linen shirt.

name: Kanzashi Hair Ornament - Fabric Flower Cluster
name_ja: かんざし つまみ細工 花クラスター
expert_note: Tsumami-zaiku is a traditional craft of folding small squares of fabric into flower petals. These kanzashi add the finishing touch that elevates a yukata outfit from casual to polished.
style_tip: Choose a kanzashi color that matches your obi, not your yukata, for the most coordinated look.

name: Uchiwa Fan - Indigo Pattern
name_ja: うちわ 藍染め柄
expert_note: Uchiwa (flat fans) are given away at summer festivals and fireworks events across Japan. A quality one with a bamboo frame and traditional print serves as both a cooling tool and an accessory.
style_tip: Tuck the uchiwa into the back of your obi when not fanning. This is the traditional carry position.

name: Kinchaku Drawstring Bag - Chirimen Crepe
name_ja: 巾着袋 ちりめん
expert_note: Kinchaku bags are the traditional carry for yukata outfits since yukata have no pockets. Chirimen crepe fabric has a distinctive pebbly texture that is quintessentially Japanese.
style_tip: Carry looped over your wrist on the same side as your dominant hand. Match the bag color to your obi.

name: Obi-Dome Accessory - Glass Bead
name_ja: 帯留め ガラスビーズ
expert_note: Obi-dome are decorative clasps worn at the center front of the obi, threaded through a thin obijime cord. They add a focal point to the outfit. Glass bead versions catch light beautifully.
style_tip: Use with a thin obijime cord over your main obi. Position at the center front, slightly below the obi's top edge.

name: Sensu Folding Fan - Bamboo and Silk
name_ja: 扇子 竹骨絹張り
expert_note: Sensu (folding fans) are more refined than uchiwa flat fans. Bamboo-ribbed with silk or washi paper covering, they fold compact for tucking into an obi or bag. A step up in elegance.
style_tip: When not in use, tuck folded sensu into the front of your obi, angled slightly to the left.

name: Women's Yukata 4-Piece Set - Lavender Bellflower
name_ja: 女性用浴衣4点セット 薄紫桔梗柄
pros: 4 pieces: yukata, obi, geta, kinchaku bag
best_for: Gift giving, first-time buyers who want everything
expert_note: The most complete set option, adding a matching kinchaku bag to the standard yukata, obi, and geta bundle. Bellflower (kikyo) is an autumn motif worn in late summer, extending the wearing season.
style_tip: Everything is pre-coordinated. Just add a kanzashi if you want to dress it up further.

name: Unisex Cotton Yukata - Classic White on Indigo
name_ja: 男女兼用綿浴衣 藍白柄
best_for: Ryokan stays, couples matching, casual home wear
expert_note: This is the type of yukata commonly provided at traditional Japanese ryokan (inns). The simple white-on-indigo pattern is timeless and works for all genders. A great everyday yukata.
style_tip: Women wrap left over right, men also left over right. Right over left is only for dressing the deceased.
